steps.prompt: Name the parts of a circle
steps.doneNote: That's the core vocabulary — you'll use these throughout the course.

targets.prompt: Tap the radius — the line from the center to a point on the circle.
targets.label: Radius
targets.hint: It starts at the center dot and stops on the circle.

targets.prompt: Tap a chord — a segment joining two points on the circle.
targets.label: Chord
targets.hint: Both ends sit on the circle, but it doesn't pass through the center.

targets.prompt: Tap the diameter — a chord that passes straight through the center.
targets.label: Diameter
targets.hint: It's the chord that runs through the center dot.

targets.prompt: Tap the secant — a line that cuts across the circle at two points and keeps going.
targets.label: Secant
targets.hint: Like a chord, but extended into a full line — note the arrows.

targets.prompt: Tap the tangent — a line that just touches the circle at a single point.
targets.label: Tangent
targets.hint: It grazes the edge at exactly one point and never goes inside.

steps.prompt: How long is the diameter compared with the radius?
steps.subtitle: The diameter runs edge to edge through the center.

options.label: The same length
options.feedback: The radius only reaches halfway — from the center to the edge.

options.label: Half as long
options.feedback: Other way around: the radius is the shorter one.

feedback.correct: Right — the diameter spans the whole circle, so diameter = 2 × radius.
feedback.incorrect: Compare how far each one reaches across the circle.
